audit_rules: Every manuscript placeholder must be generated by source code.
Every generated token choice must carry category and config-key provenance.
Every method protocol row must identify an action, evidence artifact, and output.
Every pipeline phase must identify an input, transformation, output, and guard.
Every visible configured field must be classified as explicit or defaulted.
Every disabled section must resolve to an explicit disabled-section body.
Every figure reference must be backed by a generated figure registry entry.
Every fork that adds domain claims must add domain validators and claim-ledger evidence.
Every publication claim must stay local unless a live DOI or release exists.
Every token-selection explanation must name only seed, slot, category, ordinal, and ordered category inventory as digest inputs.
Every review handoff must include generated data, reports, figures, validation results, and copy statistics alongside PDF or HTML.
Every fork migration note must name config, source, test, validator, pipeline, and claim-ledger obligations.

authoring_obligations.name: Review generated claims
authoring_obligations.obligation: Inspect hydrated manuscript bodies before copied outputs are treated as reader-ready.

authoring_obligations.name: Review config diffs
authoring_obligations.obligation: Treat lexicon, slot, title, move, and section-switch edits as source-data changes.

authoring_obligations.name: Extend claim evidence
authoring_obligations.obligation: Update the claim ledger when generated prose adds a new claim boundary.

authoring_obligations.name: Add domain validators
authoring_obligations.obligation: Add tests and validation artifacts before using the template for domain-specific claims.

authoring_obligations.name: Rerun the full project path
authoring_obligations.obligation: Regenerate analysis artifacts, render outputs, validate outputs, and copy deliverables.

authoring_obligations.name: Review method invariants
authoring_obligations.obligation: Confirm changed tokens are explained only by seed, slot, category, ordinal, or category inventory changes.

authoring_obligations.name: Assemble reviewer packet
authoring_obligations.obligation: Provide hydrated manuscript, rendered outputs, figures, data, reports, validation report, and output statistics together.

authoring_obligations.name: Write fork migration notes
authoring_obligations.obligation: Document config, source, test, validator, and claim-ledger changes required by a domain fork.

contribution_claims: A Mad Lib manuscript can remain reproducible when the lexicon is treated as data.
Conditional IMRAD section bodies can be rendered without shared renderer changes.
Large-grain manuscript variables can preserve author-readable source files while still producing a complete manuscript.
Token provenance can connect playful lexical substitution to serious publication hygiene.
A generated Methods section can be methodologically useful when protocol rows, phases, figures, and validation gates share one config-owned source.
Configured-field origin tracking can make loader defaults visible enough for reviewers and downstream forks.
Pipeline-owned output regeneration can keep PDF, HTML, slides, data, reports, and copied deliverables aligned without hand-editing generated artifacts.
A generated-method exemplar can make review handoff auditable when the review packet includes source config, data artifacts, figures, validation results, and copy statistics.
Fork migration guidance can reduce overclaiming when it names the source, test, validator, and evidence surfaces that must change before domain use.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Large-grain manuscript variables are generated from YAML and project source.
design_principles.name: Configuration owns prose choices
design_principles.rationale: Reviewers can inspect the declared language surface before generation.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: The Methods tables and body summarize method_protocol and pipeline_phases from YAML.
design_principles.name: Method surface is config-owned
design_principles.rationale: A fork should change method protocol rows before changing generated Methods prose.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Token inventory rows can be regenerated and diffed.
design_principles.name: Token choice is deterministic
design_principles.rationale: A fixed seed and lexicon must produce the same injection plan across reruns.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Configured-field tables and figures report explicit and defaulted paths.
design_principles.name: Field origin is evidence
design_principles.rationale: A generated manuscript should distinguish authored YAML fields from loader defaults.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Every disabled section resolves to an explicit disabled-section body.
design_principles.name: Sections are conditional but visible
design_principles.rationale: Disabled material should be auditable rather than silently absent.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Cover, flow, allocation, provenance, gate, and field-origin figures are regenerated from artifacts.
design_principles.name: Visual audit follows data
design_principles.rationale: Figures should explain the generated method without becoming decorative claims.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Output Markdown, PDF, HTML, and slides are rebuilt from source inputs.
design_principles.name: Generated output is disposable
design_principles.rationale: The durable artifact is the regeneration contract, not hand-edited output files.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Contribution, limitation, failure-mode, and authoring tables carry boundary text.
design_principles.name: Claim boundaries travel with prose
design_principles.rationale: Generated text can otherwise imply validation that no artifact supports.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Scope, limitations, and authoring contract require validators before domain claims.
design_principles.name: Forks must add validators
design_principles.rationale: Domain claims need domain evidence beyond this exemplar's generic regeneration gates.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Methods names the digest inputs and excludes renderer state from token choice.
design_principles.name: Invariants precede rendering
design_principles.rationale: Readers need to know which inputs are allowed to alter generated tokens before they inspect output.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Methods and reproducibility text describe review packet assembly after regeneration.
design_principles.name: Diffs are review objects
design_principles.rationale: Config, token inventory, manuscript variables, figures, and copied outputs should be diffable review surfaces.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Authoring contract and validation sections treat data, reports, figures, and logs as part of review.
design_principles.name: Review packet is a method artifact
design_principles.rationale: A PDF alone is insufficient evidence for a generated-method exemplar.

design_principles.manuscript_effect: Standalone notes, authoring obligations, and claim-ledger boundaries name fork responsibilities.
design_principles.name: Fork migration is part of the method
design_principles.rationale: A public exemplar should teach authors what must change before domain use.

evaluation_criteria.name: Placeholder resolution
evaluation_criteria.target: No unresolved uppercase manuscript placeholders remain in output/manuscript or rendered web output.

evaluation_criteria.name: Token provenance coverage
evaluation_criteria.target: Every selected token maps to category, selected value, section, and config pointer.

evaluation_criteria.name: Figure registry integrity
evaluation_criteria.target: Every referenced figure label is present in output/figures/figure_registry.json.

evaluation_criteria.name: Evidence registry cleanliness
evaluation_criteria.target: Generated manuscript numbers and claims pass the project evidence registry.

evaluation_criteria.name: Copied-output readiness
evaluation_criteria.target: PDF, HTML, slides, figures, data, and reports copy into output/templates/template_madlib.

evaluation_criteria.evidence: Stage 04 validation report and Stage 05 output_statistics.json.
evaluation_criteria.name: Reviewer packet completeness
evaluation_criteria.target: Hydrated Markdown, rendered PDF, web output, slides, figures, data, reports, validation results, and copy statistics are all present for review.

evaluation_criteria.evidence: tests/test_tokens.py and generated Methods digest prose.
evaluation_criteria.name: Method-invariant traceability
evaluation_criteria.target: Token choices can be explained only by seed, slot, category, ordinal, and ordered category inventory.

failure_modes.detection: Project test scans output/manuscript and rendered web output for uppercase placeholders.
failure_modes.mitigation: Add variable generation, tests, and rerun z_generate_manuscript_variables.py before render.
failure_modes.name: Unresolved placeholder
failure_modes.risk: A source Markdown token is added without a generated variable.

failure_modes.detection: Claim ledger review, publication metadata review, and evidence registry validation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Keep publication fields blank and use local-only claim boundaries until evidence exists.
failure_modes.name: Overclaimed generated prose
failure_modes.risk: Generated text implies a standalone DOI, empirical validation, or external release that does not exist.

failure_modes.detection: Config tests instantiate the schema and docs point to generated tables.
failure_modes.mitigation: Update config loader, docs, and tests together.
failure_modes.name: Config-source drift
failure_modes.risk: Documentation names a schema feature that source code no longer parses.

failure_modes.mitigation: Emit figure_registry.json from src.analysis before rendering.
failure_modes.name: Figure provenance gap
failure_modes.risk: A figure is referenced in manuscript output without a registry entry.

failure_modes.detection: Failure-mode table, scope text, and downstream domain validators.
failure_modes.mitigation: Add domain-specific data, validators, and claim ledgers before making domain claims.
failure_modes.name: Domain misuse
failure_modes.risk: A fork treats lexical substitution as evidence for a domain-specific research claim.

failure_modes.detection: Composition tests, summary report review, and generated method tables.
failure_modes.mitigation: Update method_protocol, pipeline_phases, composition prose, and tests together.
failure_modes.name: Method row drift
failure_modes.risk: The Methods prose describes a protocol row or phase that no longer exists in config.

failure_modes.mitigation: Expose explicit/default counts in the manuscript and review optional defaults before release.
failure_modes.name: Field-origin opacity
failure_modes.risk: A rendered field appears configured even though it came from a loader default.

failure_modes.detection: Figure registry validation, nonblank figure tests, and manual visual QA.
failure_modes.mitigation: Generate figures only from config, TokenPlan, and inventory data, then rerun validation.
failure_modes.name: Visual-method mismatch
failure_modes.risk: A figure implies a method claim that is not backed by generated data or registry metadata.

failure_modes.detection: Authoring contract review and claim ledger review.
failure_modes.mitigation: Add domain validators, domain evidence artifacts, and claim-ledger entries before asserting domain findings.
failure_modes.name: Fork without validators
failure_modes.risk: A downstream project changes vocabulary or claims but keeps only the exemplar's generic gates.

failure_modes.mitigation: Keep token selection isolated in src/tokens.py and document allowed digest inputs in Methods.
failure_modes.name: Digest invariant drift
failure_modes.risk: A future edit lets renderer state, file order, or ambient prose influence token selection.

failure_modes.detection: Claim ledger review, evidence registry validation, and documentation review.
failure_modes.mitigation: Add claim-ledger rows or remove the unsupported claim before rendering copied outputs.
failure_modes.name: Claim ledger omission
failure_modes.risk: A generated claim appears in prose or documentation without a matching local evidence row or non-claim boundary.

failure_modes.detection: Stage 05 output statistics and copied-output validation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Regenerate Stages 02-05 and include the full copied output surface in review.
failure_modes.name: Review packet incompleteness
failure_modes.risk: A reviewer receives PDF or HTML without the data, reports, figures, validation output, or copy statistics needed to inspect the method.

failure_modes.detection: README, STANDALONE notes, manuscript README, and Authoring Contract review.
failure_modes.mitigation: Document required config, source, test, claim-ledger, validator, and pipeline changes before making domain claims.
failure_modes.name: Fork migration ambiguity
failure_modes.risk: A fork leaves authors unsure which exemplar surfaces must change for a domain-specific manuscript.

method_protocol.action: Parse paper metadata and the madlib block from manuscript/config.yaml before any prose or figures are composed.
method_protocol.evidence: Config validation tests and MadlibConfig construction from the committed YAML.
method_protocol.name: Ingest declared manuscript schema

method_protocol.action: Name the manuscript scope, local claim boundary, enabled sections, and intended reviewer handoff before token generation.
method_protocol.evidence: section_plan.json, contribution table, and authoring contract rows.
method_protocol.name: Declare review scenario

method_protocol.action: Record every supported madlib path as explicit when it appears in YAML or defaulted when the loader supplies it.
method_protocol.name: Track field origin

method_protocol.action: Reject empty required categories, preserve project-owned optional categories, and treat every lexical list as source data.
method_protocol.evidence: Malformed-config tests and lexicon rows in token_inventory.json.
method_protocol.name: Govern lexicon categories

method_protocol.action: Combine seed, slot name, category, ordinal, and the full category inventory into a deterministic digest input.
method_protocol.name: Construct digest selection material

method_protocol.action: State the invariant inputs that are allowed to change token choices and the renderer state that is not allowed to affect them.
method_protocol.evidence: Token determinism tests and Methods digest prose.
method_protocol.name: Record selection invariants

method_protocol.action: Resolve every slot count into one or more token choices and assign each selected value to its configured manuscript section.
method_protocol.evidence: TokenPlan construction, provenance trace, and section allocation figure.
method_protocol.name: Expand slot declarations

method_protocol.action: Evaluate section switches before prose assembly so disabled sections receive explicit disabled-section bodies.
method_protocol.name: Apply section conditions

method_protocol.action: Build large-grain section variables from narrative moves, selected tokens, section switches, and local claim boundaries.
method_protocol.evidence: Generated manuscript variables and hydrated output/manuscript Markdown.
method_protocol.name: Compose conditional IMRAD bodies

method_protocol.action: Render method protocol, pipeline phase, design principle, audit rule, token inventory, section plan, and provenance tables from config and TokenPlan.
method_protocol.evidence: Composition tests and generated manuscript_variables.json table entries.
method_protocol.name: Assemble evidence tables

method_protocol.action: Keep generated method, visualization, and publication-boundary claims tied to config, source modules, generated artifacts, or explicit non-claim boundaries.
method_protocol.name: Align claims with evidence ledger

method_protocol.action: Write the cover overview, token-injection flow, section allocation, provenance trace, quality gate, and configured-field figures from generated data.
method_protocol.name: Generate visual audit surface

method_protocol.action: Write token inventory, section plan, configured-field inventory, injection trace, summary reports, validation inputs, and figure registry.
method_protocol.evidence: Artifact-writing tests, artifact manifest, and validation reports.
method_protocol.name: Emit machine-readable artifacts

method_protocol.action: Write manuscript_variables.json and resolve the source Markdown shells into output/manuscript.
method_protocol.name: Hydrate manuscript shell

method_protocol.action: Render PDF, HTML, and slides through shared infrastructure, then validate PDFs, Markdown, figure registry, evidence registry, design overlays, and artifact manifest.
method_protocol.evidence: Stage 03 render log and Stage 04 validation report.
method_protocol.name: Render and validate deliverables

method_protocol.action: Treat hydrated Markdown, rendered PDF, web output, slides, figures, data, reports, validation logs, and copied output statistics as one review packet.
method_protocol.evidence: Stage 04 validation report and Stage 05 output_statistics.json.
method_protocol.name: Assemble reviewer packet

method_protocol.action: Copy validated deliverables into output/templates/template_madlib only after validation passes.
method_protocol.evidence: Stage 05 copy statistics and copied-output validation.
method_protocol.name: Copy review surface

method_protocol.action: Record what downstream authors must change when moving from exemplar token injection to a domain-specific report.
method_protocol.evidence: README, STANDALONE notes, manuscript README, Authoring Contract, and claim ledger boundary rows.
method_protocol.name: Document fork migration

authoring_contract: require domain validators before domain claims
evaluation: separate local checks from publication readiness
introduction: separate playful Mad Lib syntax from research claims
identify drift between prose and source data
frame configuration as an inspectable dataset
position conditional prose as a reproducibility problem
limitations: require domain validators for domain claims
methods: declare review scenario and method invariants
separate explicit YAML fields from loader defaults
govern lexicon categories as reviewable data
expand slots through seeded digest selection
allocate slot choices to enabled manuscript sections
assemble method evidence tables and visual audit figures
align generated claims with the claim ledger
verify that no unresolved placeholders remain
assemble a reviewer packet from regenerated artifacts
preserve human review before publication claims
results: bind every manuscript token to provenance
connect the figure and inventory to the same plan

pipeline_phases.name: Schema intake
pipeline_phases.transformation: Load paper metadata and validate the madlib schema before generation.

pipeline_phases.guard: method protocol and contribution table tests
pipeline_phases.name: Scenario declaration
pipeline_phases.transformation: Summarize local scope, enabled sections, claim boundaries, and review handoff expectations.

pipeline_phases.name: Field-origin inventory
pipeline_phases.transformation: Classify supported paths as explicit or defaulted.

pipeline_phases.name: Lexicon validation
pipeline_phases.transformation: Reject empty required categories and slot references to missing categories.

pipeline_phases.name: Digest token planning
pipeline_phases.transformation: Hash seed, slot name, category, ordinal, and category inventory.

pipeline_phases.guard: token determinism and Methods prose tests
pipeline_phases.name: Invariant review
pipeline_phases.transformation: Confirm allowed token-choice inputs are documented and isolated from renderer state.

pipeline_phases.name: Slot-to-section allocation
pipeline_phases.transformation: Assign each selected token to its configured manuscript section.

pipeline_phases.name: Section composition
pipeline_phases.transformation: Build conditional section bodies, titles, and evidence tables.

pipeline_phases.name: Evidence table assembly
pipeline_phases.transformation: Render protocol, phase, audit, token, section, provenance, and configured-field tables.

pipeline_phases.guard: claim ledger and evidence registry review
pipeline_phases.name: Claim-ledger alignment
pipeline_phases.transformation: Check local claims and non-claims against source-owned evidence rows.

pipeline_phases.name: Visualization emission
pipeline_phases.transformation: Generate cover, flow, allocation, provenance, gate, and configured-field figures.

pipeline_phases.name: Artifact emission
pipeline_phases.transformation: Write inventory, section plan, injection trace, summary, cover overview, manuscript figures, and registry.

pipeline_phases.name: Render
pipeline_phases.transformation: Render PDF, HTML, and slides through the shared template pipeline.

pipeline_phases.name: Validate and copy
pipeline_phases.transformation: Validate files, registries, evidence, overlays, and copied deliverables.

pipeline_phases.input_artifact: validated project output and copy statistics
pipeline_phases.name: Review packet assembly
pipeline_phases.transformation: Group manuscript, web, slides, figures, data, reports, validation results, and copy statistics as the review surface.

pipeline_phases.input_artifact: source docs, authoring contract, and claim ledger
pipeline_phases.name: Fork contract documentation
pipeline_phases.transformation: State which config, source, test, validator, and evidence surfaces a fork must change before domain claims.

quality_probes.name: Method row completeness
quality_probes.passing_signal: method_protocol includes rows for every major pipeline responsibility.
quality_probes.question: Does the protocol table cover schema intake, token planning, composition, figures, validation, copy, and review handoff?

quality_probes.name: Field-origin visibility
quality_probes.passing_signal: Configured-field inventory and summary tables report explicit and defaulted paths.
quality_probes.question: Can a reviewer tell which visible fields were authored and which were defaulted?

quality_probes.name: Placeholder survival
quality_probes.passing_signal: No uppercase placeholders are found in generated manuscript or web files.
quality_probes.question: Did any source token survive hydration?

quality_probes.name: Provenance completeness
quality_probes.passing_signal: The injection trace and token inventory contain one row for each generated token.
quality_probes.question: Can every selected token be traced to a category, section, value, and config key?

quality_probes.name: Section-switch observability
quality_probes.passing_signal: Disabled section bodies cite their controlling section condition.
quality_probes.question: Does a disabled section resolve to a visible explanation?

quality_probes.name: Figure registry coverage
quality_probes.question: Does every manuscript figure reference have a generated registry entry?

quality_probes.name: Method-figure alignment
quality_probes.passing_signal: Figure registry captions, nonblank PNG tests, and manual visual QA align with artifact data.
quality_probes.question: Do method figures describe generated data rather than decorative or unsupported claims?

quality_probes.name: Evidence cleanliness
quality_probes.passing_signal: Evidence registry validation passes without unsupported claims.
quality_probes.question: Do generated claims stay within local evidence boundaries?

quality_probes.name: Fork readiness
quality_probes.passing_signal: Authoring obligations cite config diffs, claim ledger updates, validators, and full reruns.
quality_probes.question: Does the authoring contract tell downstream forks what to extend before adding domain claims?

quality_probes.name: Copied-output parity
quality_probes.passing_signal: Copy-stage statistics include PDF, HTML, slides, figures, data, and reports.
quality_probes.question: Did copied deliverables preserve the validated project output surface?

quality_probes.name: Digest invariant review
quality_probes.passing_signal: Methods prose names the digest inputs and token tests prove seed/category sensitivity.
quality_probes.question: Are the allowed token-selection inputs documented and protected by tests?

quality_probes.name: Claim-ledger alignment
quality_probes.passing_signal: Claim-ledger rows cover expanded method protocol and fork-validator boundaries.
quality_probes.question: Do method and documentation claims point to config, source, generated artifacts, or explicit non-claim boundaries?

quality_probes.name: Review packet completeness
quality_probes.passing_signal: Copied outputs include manuscript, web, slides, figures, data, reports, validation, and copy statistics.
quality_probes.question: Can a reviewer inspect every output surface needed to audit the method?

quality_probes.name: Fork migration sufficiency
quality_probes.passing_signal: README, STANDALONE, manuscript README, and Authoring Contract list config, source, test, validator, and claim-ledger obligations.
quality_probes.question: Does the documentation tell forks which surfaces to change before adding domain claims?
